templates.name: Application → Homework / Bonding → Booking → Show-Up Nurture
templates.summary: The follow-up for a high-ticket Application Funnel (the 'take-away sale'). After a prospect submits the application, this sequence delivers HOMEWORK / bonding videos (so they sell themselves before the call), drives them to BOOK the qualification call, then maximizes SHOW-UP and re-books no-shows. The application itself is a take-away — copy stays in 'we're selective, prove you're a fit' frame, never 'please buy'.
templates.trigger: Application form submitted (Page 2 of the Application Funnel) Contact submitted the qualification application (tag `application-submitted`)
goals: The follow-up for a high-ticket Application Funnel (the 'take-away sale'). After a prospect submits the application, this sequence delivers HOMEWORK / bonding videos (so they sell themselves before the call), drives them to BOOK the qualification call, then maximizes SHOW-UP and re-books no-shows. The application itself is a take-away — copy stays in 'we're selective, prove you're a fit' frame, never 'please buy'.
signals: Application form submitted (Page 2 of the Application Funnel) Contact submitted the qualification application (tag `application-submitted`)
Email
SMS

ghlBuild.engine: Skill 44 (caf) builds the workflow(s); GHL native calendar + Appointment Status triggers drive the booking/show-up/no-show branches.
ghlBuild.workflow_name: FSF — Application: Homework -> Book -> Show-Up -> Rebook

trigger_config.ghl_trigger: Form Submitted = application form (or Tag Added `application-submitted`)
trigger_config.qualification: If/Else on application custom fields (budget/fit) -> tag `app-qualified` vs `app-disqualified` and route disqualified to a separate path
trigger_config.calendar_triggers: GHL Appointment Status = Booked / No-Show / Cancelled drive phases C and D

actions_in_order: 1. Email + SMS — Homework / bonding video; tag `application-submitted`
2. If/Else qualification -> disqualified branch (route to lower-ticket) ; qualified continue
3. Wait 4 hours -> If/Else `appointment-booked`? FALSE -> Send Email/SMS booking nudge #1
4. Wait 1 day -> still unbooked? -> booking nudge #2
5. Wait 1 day -> still unbooked? -> booking nudge #3 (honest scarcity)
--- Separate workflow triggered by Appointment Booked: ---
B1. Send confirmation Email + SMS; tag `call-booked`; stop booking-push
B2. Wait until 1 day before -> reminder + pre-call homework
B3. Wait until 1 hour before -> SMS reminder
--- Separate workflow triggered by Appointment No-Show/Cancelled: ---
C1. Wait 1-2h -> Send 'we missed you' Email + SMS rebook CTA
C2. Wait 1 day -> second rebook nudge; after N misses -> tag `app-persistent-noshow` -> downsell/nurture
ghlBuild.goal_event: Call completed / sale -> exit nurture; route to onboarding or post-call close.
if_else_branches: qualified vs disqualified (application fields)
booked vs unbooked (Appointment Status)
showed vs no-show/cancelled
persistent no-show -> downsell
ghlBuild.notes: Cleanest as 3 linked GHL workflows: (1) application -> homework + booking push, (2) Appointment Booked -> show-up nurture, (3) Appointment No-Show/Cancelled -> rebook. caf builds each; the GHL native calendar provides Booked/No-Show/Cancelled triggers (no custom webhook needed).

templates.name: AutoWebinar (Evergreen) Watch-% Segmented Follow-Up
templates.summary: The follow-up for an EVERGREEN automated webinar that runs 24/7. Identical persuasion content to the live webinar close, but the branching is driven by WATCH PERCENTAGE / watch-time tracking instead of a fixed live date. Watch-time triggers segment registrants into: registered-no-show, watched-but-left-before-pitch, watched-through-the-pitch-no-buy, and buyer — each gets a tailored sequence. Cart-close runs on a per-registrant evergreen countdown, not a global date.

3. Wait until session start -> SMS 'starting now' + room link

8. Wait 1 day
9. Send Email — Close #2 LOGIC (The Stack)

Maps to the Cookbook 'Drop-Off Automation' Message #1/#2/#3 for the 'step-1-not-step-2' leak.

trigger_config.alternative: Tag Added = `order-step1-started` (set by the Step-1 form) used as entry; filter: contact does NOT have tag `order-completed`

actions_in_order: 1. Wait — 20 minutes
2. If/Else — condition: contact has tag `order-completed`? TRUE -> end workflow (Goal: purchase). FALSE -> continue
3. Send Email — Message #1 (friction-removal)
4. Send SMS — Message #1 mirror (optional, short link to Step 2)
5. Wait — 6 hours
6. If/Else — purchased? TRUE -> end. FALSE -> continue
7. Send Email — Message #2 (Problem/Agitate/Solve)
8. Wait — 1 day
9. If/Else — purchased? TRUE -> end. FALSE -> continue
10. Send Email — Message #3 (honest urgency + Stealth close)
11. Send SMS — Message #3 last-call mirror
12. Remove tag `order-step1-started`; add tag `cart-abandon-sequence-complete`
ghlBuild.goal_event: Purchase / `order-completed` tag — wired as a workflow GOAL so the contact exits the instant they buy (prevents emailing a customer asking them to buy what they already bought).
if_else_branches: After every wait: re-check `order-completed` -> exit on purchase
Optional: branch SMS vs email-only on contact DND / phone-present
ghlBuild.notes: GHL Automations have no public API — caf builds the workflow shape (trigger + waits + if/else + email/SMS steps); the operator pastes finalized email/SMS bodies. Honor any countdown timer used on the order page so the urgency in Msg #3 stays true.

templates.summary: Post-purchase follow-up for a Free-Plus-Shipping / Book funnel (or 2-step tripwire). trigger_config.fulfillment_branch: If/Else physical vs digital -> include shipping touch only for physical

actions_in_order: 1. Send Email + SMS — Order confirmation + bonus/access delivery; tag `fe-purchased`
2. If/Else physical? -> add shipping/tracking touch
3. If/Else `oto-declined`? TRUE -> Wait 2 hr -> Send Email + SMS second-chance OTO (Stealth close) -> If accepts tag `oto-recovered` & skip repeat
4. Wait 1 day -> Send Email — Buyer Bonding #1 (Set the Stage)
5. Wait 1 day -> Bonding #2 (High Drama)
6. Wait 1 day -> Bonding #3 (Epiphany -> backend)
7. Wait 1 day -> Bonding #4 (Hidden Benefits)
8. Wait 1 day -> Ascension Email + SMS (invite to next funnel: webinar reg / application)
9. Add tag `ready-to-ascend`; hand off to next funnel's workflow + ongoing Daily Seinfeld list
10. Refund/cancel at any point -> route to retention/cancellation-save workflow
ghlBuild.goal_event: Ascension purchase / next-funnel entry -> exit this sequence into the next funnel's follow-up.

templates.name: Membership / Continuity Stick + Retention Sequence
templates.summary: Four-phase automation that maximizes member stick rate and lifetime value: (1) rapid onboarding to get the new member to a quick win and consuming the product within hours/days, (2) monthly engagement touches that deliver the 'something new and scarce each month' Brunson cites as the core stick mechanism, (3) annual-upgrade offer timed to the renewal window, and (4) a Cancellation Funnel intercept (Survey -> Article) that saves members trying to leave. DotCom Secrets is explicit: bond with the Attractive Character FIRST, then sell continuity — waiting dramatically increases stick rate. Marketing Secrets Blackbook adds: onboarding-driven consumption is the #1 churn lever, and annual/lifetime upsells are the highest-converting offer inside a continuity program.

workflows.name: FSF — Membership: Onboarding (Phase A)
workflows.trigger: Order Submitted = membership product (or Tag Added `member-active`)
key_actions: 1. Email + SMS — Welcome + access delivery (A1); tag `member-active`
2. Wait 2 days -> If/Else `member-lesson-started`? FALSE -> Email A2 (consumption nudge). TRUE -> skip
3. Wait 2 days -> Email A3 (quick-win story + gold-nugget CTA)
4. If trial: Wait until 48h before trial end -> Email + SMS A4 (trial warning + annual offer)
5. Trial convert tag `trial-converted`; stay on monthly track

workflows.name: FSF — Membership: Monthly Engagement (Phase B)
workflows.trigger: Cron / scheduled (1st of month or content release date); fires for all contacts with `member-active` tag
key_actions: 2. Wait 14 days -> If/Else opened/clicked B1? FALSE -> Email B2 (re-engagement). TRUE -> skip
3. If/Else engaged (logged in)? TRUE -> Email B3 (streak/progress note). FALSE -> skip

workflows.name: FSF — Membership: Annual Upgrade (Phase C)
workflows.trigger: Tag Added `member-active` + Wait 30 days (monthly-billed members only)
key_actions: 1. Email C1 — Annual savings offer; tag `annual-offer-sent`
2. Wait 7 days -> If/Else `member-annual`? FALSE -> Email C2 (Stealth close, last chance). TRUE -> end
3. At offer-close date: remove annual offer; tag `annual-offer-closed`

workflows.name: FSF — Membership: Cancellation Save (Phase D)
workflows.trigger: Tag Added `cancel-intent` (set by cancel-link trigger-link click or cancel-page visit)
key_actions: 1. Email + SMS D1 — Route to Cancellation Funnel survey page; pause monthly-email workflows
2. On survey completion: branch by reason tag (`cancel-reason-price` / `cancel-reason-time` / `cancel-reason-expectation` / `cancel-reason-done` / `cancel-reason-tech`)
3. Email D2 — Personalized save email per branch
4. Wait 48h -> If/Else re-engaged or cancel-rescinded? FALSE -> Email D3 (final save + consent to cancel)
5. If still no action: process cancel tag `member-cancelled`; route to post-cancel winback nurture (Daily Seinfeld cadence)

goal_events: `member-annual` — upgrades to annual -> exit upgrade workflow
`trial-converted` — trial converts to paid -> continue on monthly track
`member-cancelled` — cancel processed -> exit all active member workflows, enter winback nurture
`cancel-rescinded` — save successful -> remove cancel-intent, resume normal engagement
ghlBuild.notes: Lesson-started / login signals come from the membership platform webhook (or a GHL trigger link inside the member area). The Cancellation Funnel survey (Phase D) is a 2-page ClickFunnels/GHL funnel — Survey Page -> Article Page — not just an email; the email in D1 routes the member to that page. The GHL 'pause' feature on workflows prevents the monthly engagement sequence from firing while a cancel-save is in progress. Annual upgrade offer must have a real close date honored; do not reopen without a legitimate reason (new content release, price change, etc.).

actions_in_order: 1. Send Email + SMS — Confirmation (Set the Stage); add tag `webinar-registered`
2. Send Email — Indoctrination #1 (next day)  [Wait 1 day between each]
3. Send Email — Indoctrination #2
4. Send Email — Indoctrination #3
5. Wait until 24h before session -> Send Email + SMS reminder
6. Wait until 1h before session -> Send SMS reminder
7. Wait until session start -> Send SMS 'We're LIVE' with join link

10. Send Email + SMS — Replay (branched) with cart-close countdown

12. Wait 1 day

templates.name: Abandoned Cart Multichannel Recovery (2-Step Order Form Drop-Off)
templates.summary: A 2-step order form collects contact info (name + email) in Step 1 before asking for payment in Step 2. If the contact completes Step 1 but not Step 2, their email is captured and a multi-channel recovery sequence can fire immediately. Brunson explicitly identifies abandoned carts as one of the largest sources of lost revenue in any sales funnel. This sequence uses email, SMS, and Messenger to bring the contact back to complete the purchase within 72 hours.
channels: Email (primary — fires immediately on cart abandon)
SMS / Text (secondary — fires if email not opened within 2h)
Facebook Messenger (tertiary — if subscribed; fires alongside or after SMS)
templates.trigger: Contact submits 2-step order form Step 1 (name + email) but does NOT submit Step 2 (payment) within a defined window (typically 15–60 minutes). Form Submitted on Step 1 order form page, combined with IF/ELSE check: tag 'purchased-[offer-id]' NOT present after wait of 30–60 minutes. Once per contact per offer (re-entry guard by tag and purchase status)

templates.name: AutoWebinar Behavioral Branching Sequence (4-Path Watch-Duration Segmentation)
templates.summary: The AutoWebinar Funnel runs the proven live webinar recording 24/7. Registrants choose a broadcast time. Four distinct behavioral segments emerge from watch behavior: (1) Registered / No-Show (never attended), (2) Left Early (attended but dropped off before the offer), (3) Watched to End / Did Not Buy, (4) Purchased. Each segment gets a different follow-up path across email + SMS + Messenger. The goal is to capture revenue from the 60–90% of registrants who typically do not buy live — the replay room and the follow-up sequences are where the majority of autowebinar revenue is made.
channels: Facebook Messenger (if subscribed — replay link, offer nudge)
Retargeting Ads (passive — visitors to webinar pages added to retargeting audiences)
templates.trigger: Webinar registration submit. All four paths are determined by watch-time data collected during the broadcast window. Form Submitted on webinar registration page. Separate triggers fire for each behavioral event: broadcast room visit, replay room visit, purchase confirmation. Once per contact per webinar run (re-entry guard by tag)

templates.name: Behavioral Retargeting — Page-Visit Pixel Fire + List Membership Update
aliases: Behavioral Ad Targeting by Funnel Stage
What More Can We Do — Retargeting Layer
templates.summary: As prospects move (or fail to move) through funnel pages, their behavior changes their retargeting audience membership in real time. Someone who visited the landing page but didn't opt in gets one ad. Someone who opted in but didn't visit the offer page gets a different ad. Someone who visited the offer page multiple times but didn't buy gets a testimonial or urgency ad. Buyers are removed from all sales retargeting immediately. This is a passive automation layer that runs in the background of every other multichannel sequence — the pixel fires are driven by page-visit events, not contact-list actions.
channels: Facebook / Instagram Retargeting Ads (custom audiences updated by page-visit events)
Google Display Network (optional second retargeting channel via Google remarketing tag)
GHL automation tags (bridge between funnel page visits and ad audience membership)
templates.trigger: Page visit to any page in the funnel stack. There is no single trigger — each page visit is its own trigger event that updates audience membership. Continuously on every page visit throughout the funnel lifetime

templates.name: Indoctrination Pre-Sell Sequence (Multichannel Story Series Before Cart Opens)
templates.summary: Before the cart opens or a sales video is released, a multichannel story sequence warms the audience so thoroughly that by the time they reach the pitch, 'the series has already done the selling' (Brunson's exact words, Marketing Secrets Blackbook Secret #65). The sequence uses 3–4 short story videos or written emails delivered across email, Messenger, SMS, and ads — each one a parable or narrative episode from the Attractive Character's life, not a product pitch. The prospect comes to know the AC's story, hardships, success, expertise, and values before seeing a single product feature.
channels: Email (primary delivery of each story installment)
Facebook Messenger (mirror of the story installment, or a short teaser that links to the email/video)
SMS (nudge only — 'Did you see [story title]? Quick link: [URL]' — not the full story)
Retargeting/Social Ads (video versions of the same story pushed as ad creative to the email list's matched audience)

Also used as an ongoing email + Messenger + ad warm-up sequence for cold audiences before a webinar or sales event. templates.name: Post-Opt-In Multichannel Stack (Email + Messenger + Desktop Push + SMS + Retargeting + Direct Mail)
templates.summary: The moment someone opts in for a lead magnet, the full multichannel stack is activated. The goal is to reach the prospect on every platform they already use — email, Facebook Messenger, desktop browser push, SMS/text, retargeting ads, and (for high-value leads) direct mail. Each channel stacks on top of the others; each additional channel makes every lead worth more. Brunson's stated metric: Messenger has 3–5x higher open rates than email; 34% of email opt-ins also subscribe to Messenger; 20% of thank-you page visitors allow desktop push. More channels = higher relationship value = more ascension up the value ladder.
channels: Facebook Messenger (opt-in checkbox on landing page — 34% adoption rate per Brunson)
Desktop Browser Push Notification (permission request on thank-you page — 20% allow rate per Brunson)
SMS / Text Message (collected at opt-in or via thank-you page micro-form)
Retargeting Ads (Facebook/Instagram pixel fires on every funnel page; list membership updated by step reached)
Direct Mail (postcards, gifts — for high-value / high-ticket follow-up)

templates.name: Abandoned Cart Recovery Sequence
channels: SMS (Step 1 immediate nudge — highest open rate for mobile users who abandoned on a phone)
Facebook/Instagram Message (optional mirror of the immediate nudge)
templates.trigger: Contact has email (captured on Step 1); tag 'checkout-started' present; tag 'buyer-[offer]' absent. The offer/cart is still open (if cart has already closed, send a 'sorry you missed it' + downsell or next-offer path instead).
signals: Contact has email (captured on Step 1); tag 'checkout-started' present; tag 'buyer-[offer]' absent. The offer/cart is still open (if cart has already closed, send a 'sorry you missed it' + downsell or next-offer path instead).
Email (all steps — primary)
SMS (Step 1 immediate nudge — highest open rate for mobile users who abandoned on a phone)
Facebook/Instagram Message (optional mirror of the immediate nudge)

ghlBuild.trigger: Workflow Trigger: 'Tag Added' = 'checkout-started' AND 'Tag Added' = 'checkout-started' with a 15–30 minute delay check (Wait 30 min → If/Else: has 'buyer-[offer]'? YES → stop. NO → start recovery).
actions: Step 0: Wait 30 minutes after trigger. If/Else: tag 'buyer-[offer]' present? YES → exit. NO → continue.
Step 1 (Immediate ~30min): Email + SMS sent simultaneously. Subject: 'Did something go wrong?' format.
Wait 24h. If/Else: tag 'buyer-[offer]' present? YES → exit buyer onboarding. NO → continue.
Step 2 (Day 1): Benefit + Guarantee email.

Wait 24h. If/Else: (a) tag 'buyer-[offer]' present → exit; (b) 'cart-close-datetime' has passed → send closed-cart email + optional downsell, then enroll Seinfeld; (c) cart still open → send Day 2 final recovery email.
Post-Day-2: If/Else: buyer → exit; non-buyer → check if cart still open → if yes merge back into launch sequence at current urgency phase; if no → enroll Seinfeld.
wait_steps: 30-minute initial hold (check if they completed purchase spontaneously).
Each wait includes a purchase-goal check (If/Else on 'buyer-[offer]') before sending the next recovery email.
if_else: PURCHASE GOAL ('buyer-[offer]') at ANY step → immediately stop all recovery emails, enroll buyer onboarding.
Cart-close-datetime passed AND no purchase → exit recovery, send 'missed it' email + optional downsell, enroll Seinfeld.
Day 2 email sent AND cart still open AND no purchase → merge back into the main launch/close sequence (whichever urgency beat is next based on cart-close-datetime).
Technical bounce on checkout link → alert operator/support queue to follow up manually (high-value contact).
ghlBuild.notes: GoHighLevel: build as a SEPARATE workflow from the main launch sequence so it can fire immediately on 'checkout-started' tag without disrupting the scheduled launch sequence timing. The purchase Goal ('buyer-[offer]') must be wired in GHL as a workflow Goal so it exits the contact from ALL active sequences when triggered. The checkout link in recovery emails should point to the same order form page (Step 1 may pre-fill via GHL contact data + URL params). Mirror the Step-1 immediate email in GHL SMS (1-2 sentence format: 'Your order is still waiting. [link]'). On mobile, many abandonments are distraction-based; SMS within 30 min recovers a meaningful portion.

ghlBuild.trigger: Workflow Trigger: 'Contact Tag Added' = 'launch-list-[offer]' (added when contact joins the warm list for this launch). Filter: tag 'buyer-[offer]' must be absent.
actions: PRE-CART: anticipation email(s) at D-5 and D-1 using 'Wait Until' the specific calendar date/time. SMS at D-1 evening.
CART-OPEN (D0): simultaneous Email + SMS + GHL Facebook/Instagram Message action + desktop push — all triggered at the launch datetime using 'Wait Until'. Add Tag 'saw-cart-open'.
DURING-CART: Day 2 logic/proof email, Day 4 guarantee/FAQ email — 'Wait Until' calendar dates.

CART-CLOSE URGENCY: T-48h email + SMS → T-24h email + SMS → T-3h email → T-1h email + SMS, all driven by 'Wait Until' the SAME cart-close datetime custom field (cart-close-datetime). The countdown timer on the sales/checkout page MUST read from the same field.
POST-CLOSE: 'If/Else' branch on tag 'buyer-[offer]': YES → enroll buyer onboarding workflow; NO → optional downsell, then enroll 'seinfeld-daily-sequence'.
wait_steps: All PRE-CART and DURING-CART waits use 'Wait Until [specific date/time]' (not relative waits) so they align with the campaign calendar.
All CART-CLOSE waits calculate backwards from the 'cart-close-datetime' custom field: T-48h, T-24h, T-3h, T-1h. This ensures email + SMS + on-page countdown timer all reference the same moment.
if_else: PURCHASE GOAL ('buyer-[offer]') at ANY step → immediately exit sequence, add buyer tag, enroll buyer onboarding, STOP all launch emails.
Branch on Cart-Open engagement: IF opened cart-open email + clicked link but no purchase → tag 'hot-no-buy', escalate SMS priority in the close cadence.

Branch on checkout started: IF 'checkout-started' tag exists without 'buyer-[offer]' → fire 'abandoned-cart-recovery' workflow immediately, do not wait for the scheduled urgency sequence (or run them in parallel if cart is still open).
Post-close non-buyer: IF a downsell offer exists → send downsell email once within 2 hours of cart-close; then enroll Seinfeld. If no downsell → enroll Seinfeld directly.
tags_custom_fields: cart-close-datetime (custom field — drives all T-minus waits + countdown timer)
ghlBuild.notes: CRITICAL: Lock the cart-close-datetime BEFORE the sequence starts. Every 'Wait Until' step, every SMS, every on-page countdown timer must reference this single field. When it passes, the offer/price genuinely changes. The fast-action bonus (if used) should have its own earlier expiry field that differs from cart-close-datetime. Build the sales page countdown in GHL Funnels using the same cart-close-datetime field. Use GHL's Facebook/Instagram messaging action for the cart-open and T-1h beats only (avoid Messenger fatigue). Sync a retargeting audience in the ad platform to mirror the cart-open period — warm-list members who visited the sales page but did not buy are your highest-value retargeting audience during the window.

ghlBuild.workflow_name: Perfect Webinar — Show-Up + Stack Close Follow-Up
ghlBuild.trigger: Workflow Trigger: 'Form Submitted' = webinar registration form → Tag 'webinar-reg-[event]'.
actions: PRE: registration confirmation email → reminder emails (24h, 1h) + SMS (1h, 'we're live') → optional 'add to calendar'.
ATTENDANCE: tag by behavior — 'attended-live', 'watched-to-stack' (watch% via webinar platform webhook), 'no-show'.
POST: same-day replay+offer email/SMS → Day1 Stack-recap (logic) → Day2 T-24h urgency → Day2 T-3h/T-1h close, all gated by a fixed cart-close datetime.
Replay page = GHL funnel page with countdown timer wired to the SAME cart-close datetime; order page with order bump + OTOs.
wait_steps: Use 'Wait until' the fixed webinar time for PRE reminders; 'Wait until' the fixed cart-close datetime for the Fear-phase beats so on-page countdown == email/SMS == reality.
if_else: Branch on attendance: 'attended-live' → shorter, hotter follow-up; 'no-show' → 'you missed it, here's the replay (24h only)' path.
Branch on watch%: 'watched-to-stack' → tag 'saw-offer', go straight to urgency cadence; 'left-before-stack' → re-pitch the offer in the recap email.
PURCHASE GOAL ('buyer-[offer]') anywhere → exit, buyer onboarding + ascend, STOP close emails.
Checkout started not completed → fire 'abandoned-cart-recovery'.
ghlBuild.notes: GoHighLevel: the on-page COUNTDOWN TIMER (replay + order page) must read from the same cart-close datetime as the Fear-phase waits — that is what makes urgency real. Build the order page with the written Stack mirroring the webinar Stack slide, an order bump, and 1–2 OTOs. Pull watch% via the webinar tool's webhook into GHL tags to drive attendance/watch branching. HONOR the close: when the timer hits zero, replay comes down and bonus/price genuinely change.

ghlBuild.trigger: Workflow Trigger: 'Tag Added' = 'start-fear-close-[offer]' OR chained automatically at the end of the 3-Closes Logic phase / SOS Day 5 click / Seinfeld 'hot-no-buy' trigger. Filter: tag 'buyer-[offer]' must be absent.
actions: Step 0: Add tag 'in-fear-close-[offer]'. Set 'cart-close-datetime' custom field if not already set.
Step 1 (Deadline Announce): Email + SMS immediately on trigger.
Wait Until [cart-close-datetime minus 48h]: T-48h email + SMS.
Wait Until [cart-close-datetime minus 24h]: T-24h email + SMS.
Wait Until [cart-close-datetime minus 3h]: T-3h email (short).
Wait Until [cart-close-datetime minus 1h]: T-1h email + SMS.
Wait Until [cart-close-datetime plus 1h]: If/Else branch — buyer vs. non-buyer.
wait_steps: All waits use 'Wait Until' the 'cart-close-datetime' custom field with offsets (minus 48h, minus 24h, minus 3h, minus 1h, plus 1h). This ensures the on-page countdown timer, the email/SMS sends, and the actual offer change all reference the SAME moment.
SMS sends should have 'send window' constraints (e.g. 8am–9pm contact timezone) to avoid late-night deliveries.
if_else: PURCHASE GOAL ('buyer-[offer]') at ANY step → immediately exit, enroll buyer onboarding, STOP all fear-close emails.
Branch post-T-48h: IF 'hot-no-buy' tag present AND checkout-started tag absent → escalate SMS priority at T-24h and T-1h.
Branch post-T-24h: IF 'checkout-started' tag present AND 'buyer-[offer]' absent → fire 'abandoned-cart-recovery' immediately (runs alongside the remaining urgency beats).
Post-close branch: buyer → onboarding + ascend; non-buyer → optional downsell (one email, one time) → 'seinfeld-daily-sequence'.
tags_custom_fields: cart-close-datetime (custom field — drives ALL 'Wait Until' steps + on-page countdown timer)
ghlBuild.notes: The 'cart-close-datetime' custom field is the single source of truth for the entire sequence. Every email/SMS wait, every countdown timer on the sales and order pages, and the actual GHL offer price change — all must reference this same field. Set it BEFORE the workflow is enrolled. When the timer hits zero, the offer/price genuinely changes in GHL (update the product price, remove the bonus, or close the order form). Use GHL's Facebook/Instagram Message action for the T-24h and T-1h beats only (Messenger fatigue is real). The T-1h SMS drives the biggest single conversion spike in most campaigns — ensure the send window allows it.

ghlBuild.trigger: Workflow Trigger: 'Contact Tag Added' = 'sos-complete'. Plus: send live broadcasts to the engaged segment from the GHL Campaigns/Email Broadcast tool.
actions: Two-mode build: (A) EVERGREEN — a workflow that drips a rotating bank of Hook/Story/Offer emails to new graduates; (B) LIVE — daily broadcasts to the 'engaged' smart list.
Each email: single CTA to a value-ladder offer; rotate the 7 episode types.
Tag click behavior (e.g. 'interested-[offer]') to feed targeted close sequences.
wait_steps: Evergreen: Wait 1 day between episodes (send window 8:00am contact tz). Live: scheduled broadcast times.
if_else: Engagement smart-list filter: include only contacts who opened ≥1 email in last 30 days (RFMS recency) so deliverability stays high.
IF a contact clicks an offer link 2+ times without buying → enroll in that offer's 'three-closes' or 'scarcity-deadline-close' workflow.
IF no open in 30–45 days → move to re-engagement workflow, then sunset to protect sender reputation.
IF purchase of offer X → suppress offer-X emails, ascend to next value-ladder offer (Stack Don't Switch).
ghlBuild.notes: GoHighLevel: maintain an 'Engaged 30-day' smart list for live broadcasts; use the Workflow for the evergreen rotation feeding new graduates. Keep ONE new opportunity and STACK offers onto it (Blackbook Secret 43) — don't introduce a brand-new opportunity each email. Publish daily (Blackbook). Each email obeys Hook/Story/Offer with exactly ONE CTA (Edwards).

ghlBuild.workflow_name: SOS — Soap Opera Sequence (5-Day Emotion Close)
ghlBuild.trigger: Workflow Trigger: 'Contact Tag Added' = 'lead-[magnet]' OR 'Form Submitted' = [opt-in form]. Filter: tag 'sos-started' is missing.
actions: Action: Add Tag 'sos-started'
Email 1 send immediately (Day 0)
Wait 1 day → Email 2
Wait 1 day → Email 3
Wait 1 day → Email 4 (+ optional SMS nudge: 'Check your email — hidden benefits inside')
Wait 1 day → Email 5 (+ SMS: 'This closes tonight: [link]')
Add Tag 'sos-complete' → triggers enrollment into Seinfeld Daily Sequence workflow
wait_steps: 5 × Wait 1 Day steps (use 'Wait until' a send-window time, e.g. 8:00am contact timezone, to keep daily rhythm)
if_else: Goal/Branch: IF 'Purchase Made' (tag 'buyer-[offer]') at ANY point → remove from SOS, enroll in buyer onboarding, STOP further close emails.
Branch on Email 1 open: IF not opened after 24h → resend Email 1 with new subject line before continuing.
Branch on Email 5 click + no purchase → enroll in 'scarcity-deadline-close' workflow for a final 24h push.
ghlBuild.notes: GoHighLevel: build as a single Workflow. Use 'Wait' steps (not drip-style 'Campaign') so the purchase Goal can short-circuit the sequence. Personalize {{contact.first_name}}. Mirror Email 2 & Email 5 to Messenger via the GHL Facebook/Instagram messaging action if a Messenger opt-in exists (DotCom/Lead Funnels multi-channel principle). Honor every deadline literally — do NOT re-open a closed cart (Edwards integrity guardrail).

templates.name: The 3 Closes Follow-Up Funnel (Emotion → Logic → Fear)
templates.trigger: Has email; has an active offer to close; not already a buyer of THIS offer.
signals: Has email; has an active offer to close; not already a buyer of THIS offer.
Email (core)
SMS (Logic recap + Fear deadline)
Facebook Messenger (mirror key beats)
Desktop push (DotCom/Lead Funnels)
Retargeting ads (open-loop reinforcement)
Direct mail (high-ticket only)

ghlBuild.trigger: Workflow Trigger: 'Tag Added' = 'start-3closes-[offer]' (set by opt-in, webinar-attended, or Seinfeld interest click). Filter: not tagged 'buyer-[offer]'.
actions: PHASE 1 EMOTION: call/embed the Soap Opera Sequence steps (Day 0–4).
Wait → PHASE 2 LOGIC: Stack email (Day 6), ROI/comparison email (Day 8), Proof+Guarantee email (Day 10), each with Buy CTA.
Wait → PHASE 3 FEAR: deadline announce (Day 12), T-48h, T-24h, T-3h, T-1h — emails + SMS, using a real countdown.
Multi-channel mirrors: GHL Facebook/IG message action on Stack + Final-Call beats; desktop push on Day 6 and T-24h; sync a retargeting audience for ad reinforcement.
wait_steps: Use 'Wait until' fixed campaign dates for the Fear phase so the deadline is REAL and shared across all channels (countdown timer on the sales page must match).
if_else: PURCHASE GOAL ('buyer-[offer]') at ANY step → exit, enroll buyer onboarding + next value-ladder offer (Stack Don't Switch), STOP all close emails.

Branch on Logic-phase engagement: IF opened Stack email but no click → resend with new subject + a single hero benefit. IF clicked sales page 2+ times no buy → tag 'hot-no-buy' and prioritize SMS in Fear phase.
Branch on Fear phase: IF clicked checkout but did not complete → fire 'abandoned-cart-recovery' workflow immediately (overrides scheduling).
Post-close branch: buyers vs non-buyers (tag-based). Non-buyers → optional 'downsell' workflow once, then 'Seinfeld Daily Sequence'.
ghlBuild.notes: GoHighLevel: build as ONE Workflow with three labeled sections (Emotion / Logic / Fear). Drive the Fear phase off a fixed campaign deadline custom field so the on-page countdown timer (sales/checkout page) and the emails/SMS all reference the SAME moment. The Purchase Goal must be wired to short-circuit from any step. Use the GHL multi-channel actions (Email, SMS, FB/IG DM, Web Push) to replicate Brunson's multi-channel follow-up funnel. HONOR the deadline — when the cart closes in GHL, the offer/price genuinely changes.

flexibility.core_principle: This template is a GUIDE and a RESOURCE, never a rule or requirement. It must not dominate the user's desire.

usage_modes.mode_1_explicit_desire: User has an explicit desire: do exactly what the user wants. This template is an optional reference only — never impose it or override the user's choice.
usage_modes.mode_2_unsure: User is unsure: suggest the 5-phase Epiphany Bridge structure (Backstory → Wall → Epiphany → Framework → Transformation) and explain why it works for personal-brand bonding, then let the user decide.
usage_modes.mode_3_just_do_it: User wants it handled ('just build it'): build the full 5–6 email sequence from this template — AC-01 through AC-06, tags, custom fields, if/else branches, video-engagement fork, and the GHL workflow — as written.

flexibility.always: Overridable, mixable, customizable, ignorable. Identity choice (Leader vs Adventurer vs Reporter-Evangelist vs Reluctant Hero), number of emails (5 vs 6), inclusion of video per email, open-loop intensity, and each storyline — all defaults the user can change or skip. The template assists; it never dominates.
